[{"note_title": "rag-systems.md", "content_preview": "# RAG Systems

Retrieval-augmented generation combines language models with external document retrieval. Instead of relying only on model memory, a retriever first finds relevant documents and gives them to the model as context.

RAG is useful when the system needs accurate, up-to-date, or private information. It is often used in question-answering systems, note assistants, and knowledge-base search tools.
", "distance": 0.7556507587432861}, {"note_title": "obsidian-note-linking.md", "content_preview": "# Obsidian Note Linking

Obsidian stores notes as Markdown files and allows users to create links between related notes. These links can turn a collection of separate notes into a connected personal knowledge graph.

An AI note assistant can improve an Obsidian vault by suggesting tags, generating summaries, and recommending related notes. Good links should be meaningful, not just based on surface-level keyword overlap.
", "distance": 0.8945478796958923}, {"note_title": "llm-basics.md", "content_preview": "# LLM Basics

Large language models are neural networks trained to predict and generate text based on large-scale language data. They learn statistical patterns from text and can perform tasks such as answering questions, summarizing documents, writing code, and reasoning over provided context.

Modern LLMs are usually based on transformer architectures. Their performance depends on model size, training data quality, context length, and how well the prompt provides useful information.
", "distance": 0.992935061454773}]

[{"note_title": "rag-systems.md", "content_preview": "# RAG Systems

Retrieval-augmented generation combines language models with external document retrieval. Instead of relying only on model memory, a retriever first finds relevant documents and gives them to the model as context.

RAG is useful when the system needs accurate, up-to-date, or private information. It is often used in question-answering systems, note assistants, and knowledge-base search tools.
", "distance": 0.851012110710144}, {"note_title": "chromadb-vector-search.md", "content_preview": "# ChromaDB Vector Search

ChromaDB is a vector database that stores text embeddings and supports similarity search. Each document is converted into a numerical vector, and related documents can be found by comparing vector distances.

In a note-taking system, ChromaDB can help find semantically related notes even when they do not share exact keywords. This makes it useful for building a linker agent that suggests connections between notes.
", "distance": 0.8945478796958923}, {"note_title": "multi-agent-systems.md", "content_preview": "# Multi-Agent LLM Systems

Multi-agent LLM systems divide a complex task across multiple specialized agents. For example, one agent may generate tags, another may summarize text, and another may retrieve related notes.

This design can improve modularity and make each step easier to inspect. However, it can also introduce extra latency, higher cost, and new failure modes when agents pass poor outputs to each other.
", "distance": 0.9989941716194153}]
summary: Obsidian enables the creation of a personal knowledge graph by linking Markdown notes together, transforming separate files into a connected system. An AI note assistant enhances this ecosystem by suggesting tags, generating summaries, and recommending meaningful connections rather than relying on surface-level keywords. To achieve these recommendations, the system might employ techniques like retrieval-augmented generation or vector search to identify semantically related content.

The attention mechanism allows transformer models to focus on the most relevant tokens in a sequence. Self-attention compares each token with every other token and helps the model understand context across the full input.
